data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T5c — Security overlay). The overlay APPLIES: this plan adds real security surface — a public HTTPS review-bot endpoint (`/review/` on 443) + Gerrit HTTP 8080 / SSH 29418, an authn boundary on inbound webhooks, and multiple credentials/grants (a GitHub deploy key, a Gerrit bot HTTP token, an EC2 instance role reading SSM SecureStrings). Verdict: sound on 3 of the 4 sub-checks; 2 grounded coaching findings, both on data-at-rest / secret-in-logs.

AFFIRMATIONS (T5c criteria this plan satisfies):
- (a) Endpoint access levels declared + sensitive paths gated: the receiver access model is explicit and sound. `/review/` on 443 is the only public surface; the stdio MCP is deliberately NOT exposed over HTTP ("Gerrit POSTs webhooks, not MCP — an MCP HTTP transport would reject the webhook"). Inbound auth is honestly scoped: the plan STATES the `webhooks` plugin "has NO native HMAC" rather than pretending a signature exists, and compensates with a secret-token URL + network ACL (S4a). Per-`(change_id, revision)` single-flight + idempotency guard the review/vote critical section against retries/backfill.
- (c) LEAST-PRIVILEGE on new grants: strong. The replication deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only to GitHub and is the sole ruleset bypass actor; the bot HTTP token's dual scope (REST `/a/` calls + git-over-HTTPS read) is documented and single-sourced; the IAM-role ownership contract (S1 CREATES the role; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies, "never re-declare the role") prevents accidental over-grant/clobber. No wildcard `*:*` / admin-for-convenience.
- (d) Secret lifecycle (largely): AWS SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; least-privilege instance-role read; the enumerated SSM param set is completeness-checked (`/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` + `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`) so no `data.aws_ssm_parameter` lookup fails on a missing param. No plaintext secrets committed to IaC (verified: `infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23` uses a `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder, not a literal).

FINDINGS (2):

- [ ] Specify encryption-at-rest (`encrypted = true` + KMS) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS volume AND its DLM snapshots — the "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet / AC1 names only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots", with NO at-rest encryption statement. That volume holds `refs/meta/config`, where the live webhook bot token is stored, and DLM snapshots INHERIT the source volume's (currently absent) encryption. Codebase confirms the gap: `infra/terraform/main.tf:27` `aws_ebs_volume "data"` sets `type = "gp3"` + `prevent_destroy` but has NO `encrypted` argument, and a repo-wide `encrypted` search under infra/ returns zero matches.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions -> "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-40
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; code fact — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) declares no `encrypted`; derisk note E1 dry-ran only the ROOT device as "gp3-encrypted", not this data volume; the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` lives on this volume (T5c(b) at-rest encryption where sensitive data is stored).
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data`; state in S1's AC that encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest.

- [ ] Address the token-in-query-string log-leak vector: the bot token is passed as a `?token=<token>` query parameter on every webhook POST (`infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23`: `url = http://review-bot:8000/webhook?token=__BOT_TOKEN__`) and on the `/review/rerun` operator path (`infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:34`). Query strings are a classic secret-in-logs sink (nginx access log, receiver request log, Gerrit webhooks-plugin delivery log). The plan bounds the token's exposure in `refs/meta/config` but does NOT state that `?token=` is redacted from access/request logs on the delivery path.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Round-11 remediation -> inbound-auth note ("inbound auth is a secret-token URL"); infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23; infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:34
  - evidence: code fact — the token travels in the URL query on both the webhook and the `/rerun` path; T5c(d) "no secret-logging"; ABSENCE of any log-redaction statement for the `?token=` query param across the nginx/receiver/webhooks-plugin log path (a repo-wide `access_log` search under infra/ returns zero matches, so no log config is pinned).
  - suggested_fix: State the redaction posture in S4a — e.g. nginx `/review/` logs a redacted URI (or omits query args) and the receiver masks the token in its request log; OR move the token to an HTTP header if the plugin supports one, keeping only the network-ACL primary gate in the URL.
